content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 5, 1988

G. Lewis Chartrand, Jr.
Nielsen, Merksamer, et al.
770 L Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-126

Dear Mr. Chartrand:

You have requested advice regarding the campaign provisions of
the Political Reform Act of 1974.1/

QUESTION

Is the name "Citizens For No-Fault, Sponsored by the
California Insurers" an appropriate name for an insurance
industry-sponsored committee supporting the proposed "No-Fault
Insurance" initiative?

CONCLUSION

The above-mentioned name meets the identification requirements
for sponsored committees under the Act at this time.

However, please note that if the measure qualifies for the
ballot, the committee must include the statement, "a committee for
Proposition ___," in all references to the committee required by
law. The committee must begin including the statement with its
name within 30 days after the Secretary of State announces the
ballot number for the measure.

In addition, the committee's statement of organization (Form
410) would have to be amended to indicate that the committee is
supporting the passage of the measure, rather than its
qualification, and to reflect the committee's new name.

_________________________________

1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code Of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Chartrand, Lewis, Jr.
Page Two

FACTS

The California insurance industry intends to form a committee
to support the proposed "No-Fault Insurance" initiative. A
majority of the contributions for this committee will come from
the insurance industry. Some funds will be solicited from the
general public.

ANALYSIS

Section 84106 requires a committee to include the name of its
sponsor in the name of the committee. In addition, Section 84107
requires that within 30 days of the designation of the numerical
order of propositions appearing on the ballot, any committee which
has as its principal activity the support of a ballot measure,
shall include the statement, "a committee for Proposition ___," in
any reference to the committee required by law.

Section 84103 requires that whenever there is a change in any
of the information contained in the statement of organization an
amendment shall be filed within 10 days to reflect the change. If
the measure qualifies for the ballot, the nature of the
committee's activities will change from supporting the
qualification of the measure to supporting the passage of the
measure. In addition, the committee's name will be changed to
include a statement that the committee supports the measure and to
include the ballot number assigned to the measure. These changes
will trigger the requirement set out in Section 84103 to amend the
committee's statement of organization.

If you have any further questions, please call me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

SAN FRANCISCO

770 L STREET, SUITE #800

650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 986-6800

SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752

MAR 25 | 5C FM 86
FILE NUMBER

March 16, 1988

6289.01

Mr. Kevin Moen
Technical Advice Unit
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95831

Re: "Citizens For No-Fault, Sponsored by
the California Insurers"

Dear Kevin:

This office represents the Insurance Industry
Initiative Campaign Committee. I am writing to confirm our
telephone conversation of March 11, 1988 in which I asked
whether the proposed name is appropriate under Title 2 Cal.
Admin. Code section 18419(b)(1).

I explained that the majority of the contributions will
come from insurance companies doing business in California. Some
of these companies are incorporated in California, and some are
incorporated in other states. All of the companies do business
in the state of California. In addition, the Committee expects
to raise some of its funds from donations from the general
public.

After discussing the issue with your supervisor, you
concluded that the above referenced name complied with relevant
regulations and statutes.

Please sign and return a copy of this letter if the
above information is consistent with your understanding of the
conversation.

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Sincerely,

G. Lewis Chartrand, Jr.

GLC:bg

Kevin Moen, Technical Assistance Unit                Date
Fair Political Practices Commission

LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

SAN FRANCISCO                                770 L STREET, SUITE 800
650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650          SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
SAN FRANCISCO CALIFORNIA 94108              TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752
TELEPHONE (415) 989-0800


                                            March 16, 1988                    6289.01


Mr. Kevin Moen
Technical Advice Unit
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95831

              Re:  "Citizens For No-Fault, Sponsored by
                   the California Insurers"

Dear Kevin:

     This office represents the Insurance Industry
Initiative Campaign Committee. I am writing to confirm our
telephone conversation of March 11, 1988 in which I asked
whether the proposed name is appropriate under Title 2 Cal.
Admin. Code section 18419(b)(1).

     I explained that the majority of the contributions will
come from insurance companies doing business in California. Some
of these companies are incorporated in California, and some are
incorporated in other states. All of the companies do business
in the state of California. In addition, the Committee expects
to raise some of its funds from donations from the general
public.

     After discussing the issue with your supervisor, you
concluded that the above referenced name complied with relevant
regulations and statutes.

     Please sign and return a copy of this letter if the
above information is consistent with your understanding of the
conversation.

     Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

                                            Sincerely,

                                            [signature]
                                            G. Lewis Chartrand, Jr.

GLC:bg


_________________________________           _________________________
Kevin Moen, Technical Assistance Unit       Date
Fair Political Practices Commission

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 28, 1988


G. Lewis Chartrand, Jr.
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson,
Parrinello & Mueller
770 L Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  88-126

Dear Mr. Chartrand:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on March 25, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Chief
                                        Technical Assistance and Analysis
                                        Division
